segments.title: Radix Hydration useId Mismatch
segments.summary: Radix hydration issue affecting Alpha 3 users due to `useId` counter divergence between server and client rendering. The problem occurs when Radix calls `useId` in SSR and the counter diverges on the client, causing hydration failures. React 19 would solve this automatically but the project is currently on React 18.
segments.retrieval_summary: radix useid counter divergence between server client rendering causing hydration failures alpha_3 users

segments.title: Old vs. New Rendering Model Explanation
segments.summary: Explanation of rendering model changes from single pass to isolated app and document rendering with streaming and stitching. The new model keeps `useId` counters aligned by isolating app render and document render, streaming both, then stitching them together.
actions: Justin: Write up the flow documentation
segments.retrieval_summary: new rendering model isolates app document rendering with streaming stitching to align useid counters

segments.title: Action Plan for Radix Hydration Fix
segments.summary: Implementation plan for shipping the Radix hydration fix using a deterministic wrapper approach. The plan includes documenting the solution, implementing the patch, testing, and keeping the wrapper behind a feature flag if needed. Specific Radix components affected include Dialog, Popover, and form controls.
actions: Justin: Push branch and draft notes
Peter: Document exact Radix components that throw
decisions: Keep wrapper behind feature flag if needed
segments.retrieval_summary: implement deterministic wrapper for radix hydration fix dialog popover form_controls behind feature_flag
